title: Pipelines, hazards, and instruction-level parallelism
description: Modern cores are deep and superscalar; data dependencies, mispredicted branches, and long-latency ops stall the pipeline and limit IPC.
body: Use when IPC is low even though the loop is small and cache-resident. Out-of-order superscalar cores execute many instructions per cycle, but a chain of dependent operations (a loop-carried reduction), a long-latency divide, or a mispredicted branch serializes execution and drops IPC. Do not assume fewer instructions means faster if they form a dependency chain. Actions: break dependency chains (multiple accumulators), hoist long-latency ops, and reduce unpredictable branches. Validation signals include higher IPC and fewer stall cycles in the top-down profile. Risk boundary: hand-scheduling can fight the out-of-order engine and the compiler; verify with counters and revert changes that do not raise IPC.
